applied_optimizations.name: 2D grid restructuring
applied_optimizations.description: Changed from 3D grid (batch, out_channels, output_length) to 2D grid (batch*out_channels, num_out_pos_blocks) to avoid HIP grid dimension limit of 65535. Each block handles BLOCK_OUT_POS=64 output positions for one (batch, oc) pair.

applied_optimizations.name: Vectorized output channels
applied_optimizations.description: Increased parallelism by processing BLOCK_OUT_CHANNELS=64 output channels per thread block using vectorized loads and 2D accumulation. Reduced runtime from 71.7ms to 6.28ms (11.4x speedup).

attempted_but_failed.name: 3D grid with output_length dimension
attempted_but_failed.error: Grid dimension z=131084 exceeds HIP limit of 65535

attempted_but_failed.name: Blocked input channels with tl.dot
attempted_but_failed.error: LLVM Translation failed for operation: builtin.unrealized_conversion_cast

top_bottlenecks: Inner loop over in_channels (32 iterations) limits parallelism
Memory bandwidth bound - loading input and weight for each kernel position
PyTorch uses highly optimized MIOpen/rocBLAS kernels that are difficult to beat with custom Triton

notes: PyTorch's ConvTranspose1d on ROCm uses MIOpen which is highly optimized
Current Triton implementation achieves 6.28ms vs PyTorch's 2.12ms (0.34x speedup)
Main bottleneck is the loop over in_channels (32) inside each thread block
Further optimization would require: (1) shared memory for weight caching, (2) better memory coalescing, (3) potentially using aiter's tuned GEMM for the underlying computation
